expressions.english: something irritating
to be irritated/irritating; to be annoyed/annoying
to show irritation; to act irritated
to show irritation; to act irritated
to annoy; to make (someone) annoyed
Why are you acting irritated like that?
He always acts irritated.; He's always irritated.
expressions.notes:
그 사람 = that person; he, she
맨날 = every day; all the time

expressions.english:
Is there something that's irritating (you)?
What's bugging you?
expressions.notes: 있어요 = to be there; to exist; to have

expressions.english: Don't be annoyed, just be pleasant about it.
expressions.notes:
즐겁다 = to be pleasant, enjoyable
즐겁게 is an adverb form of 즐겁다.
하다 = to do

expressions.english: It was a voice filled with irritation.
expressions.notes:
가득 = full, in full capacity
섞이다 = to be mixed; to blend; to be blended
목소리 = voice

expressions.korean: 그 사람은 저를 너무 짜증 나게 해요.
expressions.english: He makes me so irritated.
The baby is showing irritation. She seems hungry.
expressions.notes:
배기 = hungry
고픈다 = to be hungry
-(으)ㄴ지 = maybe because
-네요 is a sentence ending that expresses realization of a fact or agreement to a statement.

expressions.english: The elevator is stopping at every floor and it's annoying.
expressions.notes:
엘리베이터 = elevator
층 = floor
-마다 = every + N
서다 = to stand; to stop
-(으)ㄴ까 = since, because

expressions.korean: 아침부터 왜 그렇게 짜증을 내고 그래요?
expressions.english: Why have you been acting so annoyed since this morning?

expressions.korean: 몸이 안 좋아서 그런지 자꾸 짜증이 나요.
expressions.english: I keep getting irritated, and I think it's because I don't feel well.
expressions.notes:
몸 = body
안 = not
좋다 = to be good, likable; to be desirable; to be nice; to like
자꾸 = repeatedly, again and again

expressions.korean: 동생이 자꾸 제 옷을 몰래 입어서 너무 짜증 나요.
expressions.english: My younger sister keeps secretly wearing my clothes and it's so annoying!
expressions.notes:
동생 = younger sister/brother
제 = (polite) my; I
옷 = clothes, outfit
입다 = to wear

expressions.korean: 너무 그렇게 자주 짜증 내면 옆에 있는 사람도 짜증 나요.
expressions.english: If you keep acting irritated like that, people around you will also get annoyed.
expressions.notes:
자주 = often, frequently
-(으)면 = if + S + V; when/once + S + V
옆 = next (to); on the side (of); around
옆에 있는 사람 = nearby person

expressions.korean: 집안일을 아무도 안 도와줘서 혼자 다 하려니까 짜증 나요.
expressions.english: It's annoying that no one is helping with the housework and I have to do all of it alone.
expressions.notes:
집안일 = homework
아무도 = nobody; anybody (always used with a negative sentence structure)
도와주다 = to help, give a hand
혼자 = alone, by oneself
다 = all; every (thing)
-(으)려니까 = as one is about to + V

expressions.korean: 숙제가 많아서 바쁜데 동생이 자꾸 짜증 나게 해요.
expressions.english: I'm busy because I have a lot of homework, but my younger brother, who's sitting next to me, keeps bugging me.
expressions.notes:
숙제 = homework, assignment
비쌀다 = to be busy

expressions.korean: 안 그래도 늦었는데 왜 안 가져왔는 집에 다시 가야 되니까 짜증 나요.
expressions.english: I'm already late and I'm annoyed since I have to go back home because I didn't bring something.
expressions.notes:
안 그래도 = already; anyway
늦다 = to be late
뭔 = what (object)
뭔 is the contracted form of 무엇의.
가져오다 = to bring
집 = house, home
다시 = again
가다 = to go; to leave
-아/어/여 되다 = to have to + V
